Dashboard archiveTechnical Analysis hub

Combined market dashboard

The U.S. primary trend remains broad: all 15 index charts are above their 200-day averages, 14 of 15 are above both the 20-day EMA and 50-day average, and 14 of 15 have RSI above 50. Sector participation is positive but less uniform—10 of 11 sectors are above the 200-day average, 7 are above the 50-day average and 6 are above the 20-day EMA.

15 / 15Indexes above 200-day average
14 / 15Indexes above 20-day and 50-day
10 / 11Sectors above 200-day average
6 / 11Sectors above 20-day EMA
Primary trend: constructiveLeadership: XLK + XLEParticipation: unevenRotation: cyclical pockets, mixed breadth

Technology and Energy remain the only sectors outperforming SPY over the displayed year. Financials are the clearest broadening signal, while Communication Services and Consumer Discretionary are rebounding from weak bases. Technology slipping below its 20-/50-day averages, Industrials softening versus SPY and mixed defensive leadership keep breadth from becoming a synchronized expansion.

Technical interpretation only; not investment advice.

Tab 1 · US Indexes

Broad trend and participation

Source: US Indexes ChartList

15charts · 2026-07-15

Executive summary

Primary trend breadth is excellent. Every index, cap band and broad-market ETF sits above its 200-day average; only the Nasdaq 100 is marginally below both the 20-day EMA and 50-day average. Small caps, micro caps, transports and the Value Line Arithmetic Index remain constructive.

Cross-chart themes

The S&P 500, NYSE Composite, Russell 3000, Dow and S&P 100 retain positive momentum. Nasdaq 100 consolidation is the main short-term hesitation, while Utilities holding above long-term support shows that participation still includes defensive exposure.

US Indexes bottom line

The broad uptrend remains intact and well distributed across capitalization bands. A Nasdaq 100 reclaim of 29,564 followed by 30,300 would restore growth leadership, while fresh highs in the S&P 500, Value Line Arithmetic Index and small-cap benchmarks would confirm broader participation. A cluster of closes below the converged 20-/50-day supports across SPX, IWB and IWV would be the first meaningful warning.

Chart data date: 2026-07-15. ChartList count: 15. Open source page.

Tab 2 · US Sectors - Cap Weight

Leadership, participation and rotation

Source: US Sectors - Cap Weight ChartList

12charts · 2026-07-15

Executive summary

SPY remains in an uptrend. Of 11 sectors, 10 are above the 200-day average, 7 are above the 50-day average and 6 are above the 20-day EMA. Technology and Energy are the clear relative leaders; Financials are the strongest fresh breakout. The participation profile is positive but not yet uniformly broad.

Rotation and breadth

Cyclical rotation is mixed: Energy and Financials strengthen, Industrials remain constructive, but Consumer Discretionary is a major laggard and Materials is range-bound. Defensive participation is also mixed: Health Care, Utilities and Real Estate are above all major averages, while Staples is weak. Breadth is therefore stable-to-moderate rather than decisively broadening.

US Sectors bottom line

Leadership remains concentrated in Technology and Energy, while Financials provide the most credible broadening signal. Durable XLF follow-through, XLC clearing its 200-day average, XLI reclaiming relative-neutral and XLY closing above its 50-day average would confirm wider cyclical participation. Failure by XLK to hold 176.36, combined with renewed weakness in XLY/XLC, would indicate narrowing leadership.

Chart data date: 2026-07-15. ChartList count: 12 (SPY benchmark plus 11 sectors). Open source page.
